commit.message:
feat(canvas): auto-fit image to viewport on load (#13)

Compute and apply a smart initial viewport when a new image is opened:
- Images smaller than the container are displayed at 100% zoom, centered
- Images larger than the container are scaled down to fit, centered

Add `computeInitialViewport()` utility in `src/utils/viewport.ts` and
wire it into `useCanvas` via a dedicated effect that reads the container
size with `getBoundingClientRect()` to avoid React batching timing
issues.

Add unit tests covering fit-to-width, fit-to-height, exact-match,
proportional scale-down, and letterbox centering cases.

---------

commit.message:
Per-body force-based damping, solver stability, skinning fixes

Physics damping:
- Add linear_damping/angular_damping fields to phys_body_t (88→96 bytes)
- Force-based damping: F=-c*v (linear), τ=-c*ω (angular) through
  mass and inertia tensor respectively — heavier bodies resist more
- Y-component linear damping at 10% of XZ to allow natural free-fall
- Volume-scaled coefficients for ragdoll bodies: cbrt(vol/ref) scaling
  approximates cross-section differences between body parts
- Wired into tick_parallel.c (sub-substep), integrate.c, integrate_par.c
- inv_inertia_world passed to integrate args for angular damping

Solver stability:
- PHYS_ROW_FLAG_ANGULAR on all angular constraint rows
- Compliance regularization on cone-twist (α=1e-2) and hinge (α=1e-3)
- Interleaved two-pass solve: animation constraints first, structural second
- Animation constraint softness (50% effective mass for is_joint==1)
- Angular Baumgarte at 5% for flagged rows
- All fixes ported to parallel solver (tgs_solve_par.c)

Skinning fixes:
- FVMA exporter forces armature to rest pose before mesh evaluation
  (fixes mesh baked in posed state vs rest-pose IBMs)
- Bone weight normalization at engine load time in skeletal_mesh_fvma.c
  (Blender raw weights don't sum to 1.0; shader requires it)

* refactor: comprehensive codebase reorganization into modular structure

Major refactoring to improve code organization, maintainability, and clarity:

STRUCTURE CHANGES:
- Created src/ module hierarchy with clear separation of concerns
- Organized into: config, domain, strategies, scanning, position_management,
  data, notifications, analysis, utils

- src/config/settings.py: Central configuration (single source of truth)
- src/domain/models.py: Dataclasses for Signal, Position, Trade
- src/strategies/: Strategy implementations with base.py and registry.py
- src/data/: Market data fetching, indicators, historical data caching
- src/scanning/: Scanner logic and pre-buy validation
- src/position_management/: Position tracking, monitoring, exits
- src/notifications/: Email alerts with templates and formatters
- src/analysis/: Backtesting, metrics, diagnostics

SCRIPTS CREATED (5):
- scripts/scan.py: Live scanner with CLI
- scripts/backtest.py: Historical backtester
- scripts/monitor.py: Position monitor
- scripts/manage_positions.py: Position management CLI
- scripts/download_data.py: Data downloader utility

DOCUMENTATION CREATED:
- docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: System architecture and design
- docs/API.md: Complete API reference
- docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: Development guidelines
- Updated README.md with new structure

TEST INFRASTRUCTURE:
- Reorganized into unit/, integration/, fixtures/
- Created conftest.py with 20+ pytest fixtures
- Added mock classes for testing without network
- Created sample data generators

BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY:
- Old files remain in place (utils/, config/, scanners/, core/)
- Can import from both old and new locations
- Gradual migration possible for existing code

Total files affected: 80+
New modules: 30+
Documentation: 4 comprehensive guides
Entry points: 5 professional scripts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add RS Ranker bought ticker tracker with cooldown

- Create RSBoughtTracker class to maintain bought ticker list
- Prevent duplicate BUY signals for already-recommended tickers
- Implement 30-day cooldown before allowing re-entry of closed positions
- Support position tracking: bought → pyramided → closed lifecycle
- Add persistent JSON storage (data/rs_ranker_bought.json)
- Integrate tracker with scanner for automatic signal recording
- Support backtest dates for walk-forward testing
- Initialize fresh tracker for each backtest run

Features:
- is_bought(ticker): Check if ticker is in active position
- can_buy_again(ticker, cooldown_days=30, as_of_date=None): Check if closed
  ticker can be recommended again (cooldown expired)
- add_pyramid(ticker, date, price, size_pct): Record pyramid adds
- close_position(ticker, exit_date, exit_price, exit_reason): Record exits
- get_summary(): Get performance summary

Addresses:
- No more duplicate buy emails for same ticker
- Realistic cooldown period prevents whipsaw trades
- Can re-enter same ticker after 30 days if setup improves
- Full backtesting compatibility with date-aware cooldown

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: pass tracker to scanner for persistent backtest tracking

- Modify run_scan_as_of() to accept optional rs_bought_tracker parameter
- Backtester now passes its tracker instance to scanner
- Enables persistent duplicate prevention across all scan days
- Realistic simulation: same ticker won't get duplicate signals during backtest
- Fresh tracker for live trading (when called without tracker param)

This ensures:
- Backtest: Tracker persists across all scan days (realistic)
- Live: Fresh tracker per scan (stateless for production)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle re-entry of closed tickers in tracker

When a closed ticker meets criteria again (after cooldown expires):
- Previously: add_bought() wouldn't update existing ticker data
- Now: Overwrites old closed data with new entry info
- Result: Fresh position tracking for re-entries

Scenario:
- Day 1: AAPL exits → status='closed'
- Day 35: AAPL meets criteria again (cooldown expired)
- Scanner generates signal + calls add_bought()
- Tracker resets: status='bought', new entry_date/price, pyramids=[]
- Realistic simulation of re-entry after cooldown

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  When RelativeStrength_Ranker_Position exits, tracker now records: exit_date, exit_price, exit_reason, profit_loss
  This enables cooldown logic: closed positions cannot be re-entered for 30 days

  Enables tracking of position evolution: bought → pyramided → closed

  Created persistent rs_bought_tracker instance at module level
  Pass tracker to run_scan_as_of() so each live scan uses same tracker instance

fix: context-dependent button foreground for WCAG-compliant contrast (#112)

Co-authored-by: Anselmoo <13209783+Anselmoo@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: address review feedback on pickReadableForeground

Co-authored-by: Anselmoo <13209783+Anselmoo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: guarantee WCAG AA on buttons by falling back to #000000/#ffffff

pickReadableForeground previously selected the best of two palette
colors but could not guarantee WCAG AA (4.5:1) when mid-luminance
accent backgrounds (e.g. teal #0085a7) left both fg0 (~2.81) and
bg0 (~4.37) below the threshold.

The updated logic:
1. Evaluates all palette candidates as before.
2. Returns the highest-contrast palette color when it meets 4.5:1.
3. When neither palette option reaches 4.5:1, falls back to whichever
   of pure black (#000000) or pure white (#ffffff) scores higher —
   which is mathematically guaranteed to achieve WCAG AA for any
   background luminance.

- Updated mid-luminance test to assert >=4.5 (was 'best of two')
- Added contrast.test.ts with unit coverage for pickReadableForeground
  including the AA-guarantee invariant across 10 background values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

hud: protocol-aware FPS cap and 2-decimal internal precision

Add fps_cap float to _MJPEGHTTPServer and MJPEGServer.start(). Expose
fps_cap in /status JSON so JS can derive all thresholds proportionally
(green >= cap*0.8, orange >= cap*0.4) rather than hard-coding RTSPS values.

Camera tool injects cap per protocol at stream start:
- tcp_tls (A1/P1): 0.5  — green at >=0.4 fps, orange at >=0.2
- rtsps (H2D/X1): 30   — existing behavior unchanged (green>=24, orange>=12)

Backend rounds fps to 2 decimal places (was 1). Display uses toFixed(1)
for sub-2 fps values since the 10s rolling window makes 0.1 the natural
measurement granularity.

commit.message:
Add error handling for unknown CLI options

Previously, the CLI would silently ignore unknown options like --unknown
or typos like --typcheck, which could lead to confusing behavior where
users thought they were using an option that was actually being ignored.

Now, any unrecognized --option or -option (with length > 1) will cause
the CLI to exit with error code 1 and display all unknown options to the
user.

  any remaining --/- prefixed arguments as unknown options
- Added three new integration tests to verify the behavior:
  - Unknown single option causes error

commit.message:
fix: remove cancelOrphaned() that prevented timers from firing

cancelOrphaned() in TimerRegistry.start() marked ALL pending timers
as 'cancelled' in the DB before loadPending() ran, so the in-memory
pending map was always rebuilt empty. This broke:
- Timers created before start() (13 test failures)
- Timer persistence across registry restarts
- Repeat timer rescheduling

The fix removes cancelOrphaned() entirely. Stale timer cleanup is
already handled by cleanupOld() (7-day TTL), and fired timers for
non-existent agents are harmlessly ignored in the event handler.

Also changed loadPending() to merge with existing in-memory timers
rather than clearing first, so timers created before start() are
preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve merge conflict in main.jsx and harden setup.sh

  keep HEAD version with RemoteDesktopPage import and route

- Harden setup.sh for all Linux distributions:
  - Add $SUDO wrapper (works as root or with sudo, no sudo not fatal)
  - Add check_merge_conflicts() guard before any build step
  - Dynamic Go version fetch from go.dev/dl at runtime

  - ARM / i386 arch support in install_go()
